# dokploy_host_schedule (Resource)

A cron-scheduled command run on the Dokploy host (the machine running Dokploy itself). Use this for instance-wide jobs.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "dokploy_host_schedule" "rotate_logs" {
  name            = "rotate-traefik-logs"
  cron_expression = "0 0 * * *"
  command         = "find /var/log/dokploy -name '*.log.*' -mtime +14 -delete"
  timezone        = "America/Sao_Paulo"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `command` (String) Shell command to run.
- `cron_expression` (String) Cron expression.
- `name` (String) Display name.

### Optional

- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the schedule is active. Defaults to `true`.
- `shell_type` (String) Shell. Defaults to `bash`.
- `timezone` (String) IANA timezone.

### Read-Only

- `app_name` (String) Internal name generated by Dokploy.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import dokploy_host_schedule.rotate_logs <scheduleId>
```
